document_extractions.legal_description_full:
FIRST TRACT: BEGINNING at a point marking the intersection of the north technical property line of West Martin Street with the west technical property line of South Dawson Street, said point of beginning being 33 feet north of the technical center line of West Martin Street and 33 feet west of the technical center line of South Dawson Street; runs thence in a northerly direction along the west technical property line of South Dawson Street and in a line parallel to and 33 feet west of the technical center line of South Dawson Street 126.71 feet to a point in the dividing line between the property of the City of Raleigh and the property herein described; runs thence in a westerly direction in a line almost perpendicular to the west technical property line of South Dawson Street a distance of 4.93 feet to the north face of the north wall of a brick building known as the Dillon-McLeod Building; thence continuing in a westerly direction along the north face of the north wall of said building (being in a line almost perpendicular to the west technical property line of South Dawson Street) a distance of 41.96 feet to a point evidenced by an offset in said north wall of the Dillon-McLeod Building; runs thence in a southerly direction along the west face of said wall at said offset a distance of 0.19 feet to a point; runs thence in a westerly direction along the north face of the north wall of said Dillon-McLeod Building (being in a line almost perpendicular to the west technical property line of South Dawson Street) 184.36 feet to a point evidenced by an iron pipe; runs thence in a northerly direction along the dividing line between the City of Raleigh property and the Dillon-McLeod Building property 49.59 feet to an iron pipe, a common corner with the City of Raleigh property and the Dillon-McLeod Building property; runs thence in a westerly direction in a line making an interior angle of 90 degrees with the preceding line and being along the dividing line between the property formerly owned by Dillon Supply Company and the property of the City of Raleigh as shown on the map showing the dividing line recorded in Book of Maps 1952, page 26, Wake County Registry, 188.75 feet to the east technical property line of South Harrington Street; runs thence in a southerly direction along the east technical property line of South Harrington Street 176 feet to a point marking the intersection of the east technical property line of South Harrington Street with the north technical property line of West Martin Street; runs thence in an easterly direction along the north technical property line of West Martin Street 420 feet to the point and place of BEGINNING.

SECOND TRACT: BEGINNING at the point of intersection of the north technical property line of West Martin Street and the west technical property line of South Dawson Street, said point being 33 feet north of the technical center line of West Martin Street and 33 feet west of the technical center line of South Dawson Street, runs thence in a westerly direction along the south line of the First Tract above described and being along said north technical property line of West Martin Street, a distance of 420 feet to the intersection of the east technical property line of South Harrington Street with the north technical property line of West Martin Street; runs thence in a southerly direction along the east technical property line of South Harrington Street 5.39 feet to a point in the north visible property line of West Martin Street; runs thence in an easterly direction along said north visible property line of West Martin Street 420 feet, more or less, to the point of intersection of said north visible property line of West Martin Street with the west technical property line of South Dawson Street; runs thence in a northerly direction along said west technical property line of South Dawson Street a distance of 4.75 feet, more or less, to the point and place of BEGINNING.
document_extractions.legal_description_short: Two tracts in the City of Raleigh, Wake County, NC bounded by West Martin Street, South Dawson Street, South Harrington Street, and the Dillon-McLeod Building; described by metes and bounds.

document_extractions.legal_description_full: 1st. Tract: BEGINNING at the rock ford (White Oak Creek) in C. J. Burt's corner and runs thence up the various courses of said creek to a stake and pointers, corner of lot No. 4; thence 3/4 deg. East 9 chains to a stake and pointers in the line of lot No. 4 corner of lot No. 5; thence North 89 deg. West 16 chains to a stake and pointers in said Burt line; corner of lot No. 5; thence South 5½ deg. West 17.20 chains to the point of beginning, containing 25.17 acres, more or less, and being the same land which was conveyed to Rudolph J. Baker by J. E. Pearson, Commissioner by deed dated October 11, 1930 and recorded in Book 608 at page 217, Wake County Registry. See Book 857 page 449, Wake County Registry, said property herein being lot or tract No. TWO of said deed. 2nd. tract: That tract or parcel of land situated in Buckhorn Township, Wake County, North Carolina, same being lot No. 4 in the Division of the lands of Nancy E. Williams, deceased, containing 14.77 acres, more or less. For a more complete description of said land reference is hereby made to Book 'D' of partitions Page 429, Office of the Register of Deeds for Wake County, North Carolina. Being the same identical land described in a deed dated April 22, 1919, by Clarence J. Smith, Trustee, to J. A. Hockaday, and recorded in Book 338 at page 597, in Wake County Registry. This is the same land that was conveyed to Rudolph J. Baker by J. A. Hockaday and wife Florence Hockaday by deed dated August 20, 1930, and recorded in Book 592 page 426, Wake County Registry. See also Book 857 page 449, Wake County Registry. 3rd. tract: Lying and being in Buckhorn Township, Wake County, and being lot No. 3 as shown on the plat and survey made by C. H. Collins, surveyor, February 1914, attached to the report of Commissioners in Special Proceedings, No. 2031, Record of Partitions 'D' Page 443, in the office of Clerk of the Superior Court, Wake County, and described as follows: BEGINNING at a stake and pointers in F. C. Adams line, corner of lot No. 2 running thence South 3/4 deg. West 2.68 chains to an old field pine, said Adams corner in the line of lot No. 5; thence South 89 deg. East 2.65 chains to a stake and pointers corner of lot No. 5; thence South 3/4 deg. West 8.03 chains to a stake and pointers in the line of lot No. 5 corner of lot No. 4; thence South 87 deg. East 20.55 chains to a stake and pointers just on the West side of the road; thence North 7 deg. East with said road 10.65 chains to a stake and pointers corner of lot No. 2; thence North 87 deg. West 24 chains to the beginning, containing 23.07 acres, more or less, and being the same land allotted to J. C. Williams in the partition of the lands of Nancy Williams by Commissioner C. H. Collins, C. J. Burt and G. H. Holloway in the Special Proceedings before referred to, and being the same land which was conveyed to Rudolph J. Baker by Wm. B. Oliver and wife, see Book 608 page 220, Wake County Registry. [Second deed on pages 3-5: W. W. Holding and wife Josephine H. Holding to Lydia Hayes — lot in Wake Forest, Wake County, NC, on North Side of Fort Street, Dennis Kearney's corner, running Easterly 60 feet; thence Northerly along J. R. Mangum's line 120 feet and 3 inches; thence Westerly along Will Debnam's line 60 feet to Dennis Kearney's line; thence Southerly 120 feet and 3 inches to point of BEGINNING, together with right to use of a well situated between the above described lot and a lot belonging to J. R. Mangum.]
